---
title: "Understanding Oesch"
format: html
editor: visual
execute: 
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

## 

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| 
library(cesdata2)
source("1_housing_class_master.R")
```

This is implementation of the Oesch 16-class categorization, where I have lumped all the self-employed together. He distinguishes those. 
```{r}
ces25b %>% 
  filter(!is.na(occupation_oesch)) %>% 
  filter(employment==1 )%>% 
slice_sample(n=5, by=occupation_oesch) %>% 
  as_factor() %>% 
  select(occupation_name, NOC21_5, occupation_oesch,occupation_oesch_5) %>% 
  knitr::kable()
```

This is the distribution of respondents on that schema..

```{r}
ces25b %>% 
  count(occupation_oesch) %>% 
  as_factor() %>% 
  knitr::kable()

```

Ths is the distribution of employment status. 
```{r}
ces25b %>% 
  count(cps25_employment) %>% 
  as_factor() %>% 
  knitr::kable()

```
